title: Mildly Sour Irish Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful Irish stew featuring lamb, sage, and a hint of sourness, cooked to perfection in a clay pot. This paleo-friendly dish is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb cubes with salt and black pepper.
Heat a clay pot over medium heat and brown the lamb cubes on all sides.
Add the chopped onions to the pot and cook until they are softened.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes to the pot and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the chopped sage, apple cider vinegar, and beef broth to the pot and stir to combine.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Cook the stew for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the vegetables are cooked through.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
